etymology.description: 生(生) (shēng, "life; give birth") + 田(田) (tián, "field") = phonetic and semantic elements
etymology.interpretation: The character 甡(shēn, "broom") is a phono-semantic compound that combines 生(生) (shēng, "life; give birth") as a phonetic component with 田(田) (tián, "field") as a semantic element. The semantic link may relate to the notion of managing or cleaning a field, although the exact reason for its use as "broom" isn't explicit. Often, phono-semantic compounds relied on established idiomatic extensions rather than direct visual or conceptual links.
